what_it_does: Records a noticeboard comment by tim that endorses an iOS context-menu interaction pattern and floats the idea of creating a surface capability registry for iOS.
observations: It is a note of type 'note' in state 'posted' on the dragnet-development channel
The body quotes a prior recommendation: tap commits the default block; long-press escalates to a scrubber + command bar
It cites iOS's UIContextMenu / long-press preview + action list as a known platform idiom
It mentions making default-block detection 'smart' so tap is right most of the time
Tim agrees with the reasoning and suggests starting a 'surface capability registry or something' for iOS
standouts: Explicit endorsement of the iOS context-menu / tap-vs-long-press interaction model
Proposal to create a 'surface capability registry' for iOS mechanisms
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated, and history
Uses the '[paragraph] re: «...»' quoting convention seen in noticeboard notes
References another board item via a 'commented_on' link
concerns: The phrase 'surface capability registry or something' is vague and leaves the concept undefined
No owner, next step, or definition is attached to the registry idea
notes: Tim's note agreeing with the iOS long-press escalation pattern and seeding the idea of an iOS surface capability registry.
questions: What exactly is meant by a 'surface capability registry'?
What does 'mechanism support' refer to in this context?
What is the 'scrubber + command bar' that long-press should escalate to?
What constitutes the 'smart' block parsing mentioned?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a posted note/comment; actual content is agreement plus a design proposal, which matches the note format.
apparent_themes: Smart defaults reducing need for advanced actions
intention_signals: To capture design consensus around an iOS-specific interaction pattern and to plant the idea of a structured registry for iOS surface capabilities.
novelty: The suggestion of a 'surface capability registry' as a way to organize iOS-specific interaction mechanisms.
contribution: Preserves an endorsement of a platform-idiomatic iOS interaction model and an early, unshaped concept of a surface capability registry that a fused system could retain for later elaboration.
summary_for_embedding: A posted noticeboard comment by tim on item-438d2f67 endorsing an iOS context-menu tap/long-press interaction pattern and suggesting an iOS surface capability registry.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement with a proposed iOS interaction model and to propose a registry for iOS surface capabilities.
